_comment: Recall-benchmark budgets for the recall-bench harness (#9956). The harness drives the REAL @elizaos/core recall pipeline over a deterministic templated corpus and compares MEASURED rows against these budgets, exiting non-zero when a hard budget is crossed. recallAt5 / ndcgAt10 are quality FLOORS (>=); latencyMsP95 is a CEILING (<=). A skipped (unmeasured) row never fails a budget. Vector and hybrid clear a HIGHER recall floor than keyword (the embedding path's semantic recall earns its keep — measured standard-tier baseline: hybrid/vector recall@5=0.55 vs keyword=0.47; runtime-vector pure cosine recall@5=0.79). selfCheck.minRecallAt5 is the floor the deterministic concept-hash self-check (hybrid mode) must hit to prove the pipeline retrieves at all; it can never satisfy a real per-mode quality gate. failOpen.maxRecallDeltaAt5 is the maximum allowed |hybrid/vector recall@5 - keyword recall@5| when the query embed is forced to throw: the fail-open path MUST collapse hybrid+vector to the keyword baseline, so the delta must be ~0. Floors/ceilings were set from the standard-tier baseline (.github/issue-evidence/9956-recall-bench/) with margin; ratchet them as the recall pipeline improves.
